# Principle 9: Stress-Test Assumptions


> **If your Skill works because of a model limitation, revisit every assumption after a model upgrade.**

Thariq explains this principle in "Seeing like an Agent" using the TodoWrite → Tasks evolution:

Early Claude Code used a TodoWrite tool to let Claude maintain a task list. Anthropic even injected a system reminder every 5 turns so Claude wouldn't lose track of its goals. This design was calibrated to the limitations of the model at the time.

As models improved, two problems emerged. First, the reminders started constraining Claude — it felt it had to follow the todo list rather than adapt it. Second, stronger Subagent orchestration became possible, but the old structure couldn't support it. Anthropic replaced TodoWrite with Tasks.

**What to review:**
- Identify which constraints in your Skill are compensating for current model limitations — if the model has gotten stronger, remove them.
- Confirm whether those constraints are now active obstacles rather than aids — if so, remove them immediately.
- After every model upgrade, systematically audit all assumptions in your existing Skills.

**Bad**: "Our Skill is working fine. We'll leave it alone when the model changes." — Depending on luck, not principles.

**Good**: When the model version changes, review each critical Skill and ask: "Which parts of this Skill were compensating for the old model's weaknesses?"
